proposed_project_information.description_of_proposed_project:
Lakeside Commons is a proposed mixed-use development that will combine neighborhood-scale retail, professional office space, and medium-density residential units on an approximately 7.8-acre site along Eldorado Parkway in Little Elm. The concept includes two, two-story mixed-use buildings fronting the primary roadway with ground-floor retail/restaurant uses (approximately 22,000 sq. ft. total) and second-floor office space (approximately 18,000 sq. ft.). Behind the commercial frontage, the project will introduce three small-scale, three-story multifamily buildings (approximately 96 units total) oriented around shared green spaces, internal pedestrian pathways, and a small central plaza.

The intent is to create a walkable, neighborhood-serving center that complements nearby single-family neighborhoods and provides convenient dining, services, and small business office space within the Town of Little Elm. The residential component is envisioned as a mix of one- and two-bedroom apartments targeting young professionals, small families, and downsizing empty nesters who want proximity to local amenities and the lake. Parking will be a combination of surface lots and limited covered parking, with a focus on clear pedestrian connections between buildings, sidewalks along the public streets, and safe access to nearby transit stops.

The project will incorporate high-quality architectural materials (brick, stone, fiber-cement siding, and metal accents) consistent with the Town’s design standards. Landscaping will emphasize native and drought-tolerant species, enhanced streetscape plantings, and shade trees along pedestrian routes. Stormwater will be managed through a combination of underground detention and on-site bioretention areas, with an emphasis on minimizing runoff impacts to adjacent properties. The development is planned to be constructed in two phases: Phase I focusing on the commercial buildings and associated parking and infrastructure, and Phase II completing the multifamily buildings and remaining site improvements.

We anticipate that Lakeside Commons will support approximately 60–80 permanent jobs (retail, restaurant, and office tenants) and provide additional housing options within a short drive of the lake, schools, and existing town facilities. The project team is seeking early feedback from Development Services on zoning compliance, traffic circulation, utility capacity, drainage requirements, and architectural expectations before finalizing the formal zoning/site plan submittal.
proposed_project_information.location: Approx. 3100–3200 block of W Eldorado Parkway, Little Elm, TX 75068 (north side of Eldorado Pkwy, east of FM 423, within Little Elm town limits)

project_team.please_list_the_members_of_your_team_exp:
1. Sarah Kim, AIA – Project Architect, Kim + Turner Architects, 901 Elm Street, Suite 600, Dallas, TX 75202, (214) 555-9134, skim@kt-architects.com. Sarah is leading the site planning, building design, and coordination with the Town’s architectural standards. She will be prepared to walk through the preliminary site layout, building massing, parking configuration, and how we are addressing the Eldorado Parkway frontage.

2. Carlos Mendoza, P.E. – Civil Engineer, Northlake Civil Engineering, 1420 Lakeside Parkway, Suite 210, Flower Mound, TX 75028, (972) 555-4412, cmendoza@northlakecivil.com. Carlos is responsible for site civil design, including grading, drainage, water and sewer connections, and driveway access. He will bring preliminary utility capacity assumptions, a draft drainage approach (including preliminary detention sizing), and traffic circulation concepts for review.

3. Jennifer Lewis – Land Use Attorney, Lewis & Hartley, PLLC, 200 Crescent Court, Suite 1650, Dallas, TX 75201, (214) 555-7720, jlewis@lewishartleylaw.com. Jennifer is assisting with zoning interpretation, any potential need for a Planned Development (PD) overlay, and coordination of any required public hearings. She will be available to discuss the existing zoning, proposed deviations (if any), and recommended path forward for entitlements.

4. David Patel – Traffic Consultant, P.E., North Texas Mobility Group, 5081 W University Drive, Suite 300, Denton, TX 76207, (940) 555-3389, dpatel@ntmobilitygroup.com. David is preparing the Traffic Impact Analysis (TIA) and will be present to discuss anticipated trip generation, access management on Eldorado Parkway, potential turn lane requirements, and any needed coordination with TxDOT or Denton County.

5. Emily Rogers – Development Manager, Reynolds Development Group, 4100 Spring Creek Parkway, Suite 250, Plano, TX 75024, (469) 555-2890, erogers@reynoldsdevelopmentgroup.com. Emily is my internal project lead and will be responsible for day-to-day coordination with Town staff. She will take detailed notes on requested revisions, submittal timelines, and follow-up items to make sure we are aligned with the Town’s expectations.
